Mrs. Stephenson’s tragic death has nothing to do with a 2002EU directive, which by the way was replaced by Regulation EU 168/2013. This regulation is all about enhancing safety requirements applicable for (electric) motorbikes. And these safety requirements are strict. The real issue is that some drivers are circumventing these regulations. A law is not effective if there is no enforcement. So I hope that, instead of dragging EU regulations into the death of Mrs. Stephenson, the focus of preventing this kind of accidents will be enforcement of the law and prevention of anti social behaviour. J. Scott.

I was diagnosed with osteoporosis in my late 30s. I was prescribed HRT patches until there was an unfounded cancer scare linked to them, resulting in my GP refusing to prescribe anyone HRT. I was offered alendronic acid by infusion and Denosumab, but rejected both due to the possible side effects. I’ve been taking Vit D and Glucosamine, I also go to Pilates and my bone density has improved. Posture, staying fit, keeping mobile and eating sensibly is very important whether someone has any form of bone deterioration, or not. I’m now 74 and feel quite fit and well.

Nearly 3.5million people in the UK live with osteoporosis, characterised by weakened bones and predominantly affecting postmenopausal women.
  • 8 comments
  • share Women with brittle bones are 50 per cent more likely to die early

born in 1947 along with the most people born after the war, my family had to scrape by like most others, but then as things improved, looking back I think we had the best of our lives then. Plenty of work, lots of us bought our own homes, most people law abiding and honest, no riots as people got on with others, the sad thing being, those times will never again happen. I really feel sorry for my grandchildren as we lived in the golden years which will never return.

To a time traveller from the post-war era, Britain today is a fundamentally different place from what it was. All the old certainties are gone.

A humpback whale found dead this week off a Danish island has been identified as Timmy the whale - the animal released as part of a £1.3million rescue mission. Timmy whose health had severely deteriorated after being stranded near Germany since March - was released on May 2 after being transported in a water-filled barge by rescuers. But just days after Timmy entered the North Sea off Denmark, and following a lack of tracking data, the German Oceanographic Museum determined he didn't survive the transition to deep water.

I have literally just had surgery to remove ovarian cancer and I want all females to know these things 1. I had literally no symptoms of ovarian cancer _ nothing! I went to the doctor because I could feel a lump in my abdomen. I had stage 3 high grade cancer in both ovaries, appendix and the abdominal wall. So yes, screening is necessary but ladies, check your abdomen as often as you check your boobs. 2. Even though you have to pay have a BRCA test. I was negative but if you are positive you can have elective surgery 3. I had no reason why I should get ovarian cancer in terms of lifestyle, heredity etc. I did however, in the past use a lot of talc which I now know contained asbestos. I believe this was the cause. LADIES, DO NOT USE TALC and if you have used it in the past get a test. Ovarian cancer has a high survival rate if it is found early enough. This is the single most important issue and I will be spreading this word for as long as I live!!!

Ovarian cancer has long been dubbed a 'silent killer' because its symptoms are all too easy to overlook.
  • 14 comments
  • share The ovarian cancer signs women can't ignore from bloating to bleeding

German boxer Viktor Jurk scored one of the fastest knockouts in history, flooring Edwin Castillo with one punch just seconds into their bout on Friday night. The unbeaten fighter came into the fight with a significant amount of hype in his homeland, and just moments after the bell rang in Mannheim, he showed what he was capable of. Brooks Nader channels Pamela Anderson as she slips into iconic red swimsuit for Baywatch reboot

Brooks Nader channelled Pamela Anderson as she slipped into the iconic red swimsuit while filming Fox's Baywatch reboot in Los Angeles on Friday. The actress, 29, showcased her incredible figure in the high-leg swimsuit and had her hair styled in a bouncy blowdry. Set on the shores of Los Angeles, the Baywatch reboot is highly anticipated and also features some of the old timers such as Erika Eleniak and David Chokachi.

FA Cup final Day. not one story, absolutely zero build up for an event that used to obses the entire Country for weeks beforehand and brought it to near standstill on the day. Endless 'pick a team', pick a strip etc nonsense, not one single bit of build up for the Cup Final.

Germany's home strip pays tribute to their 1990 and 2014 designs, which might make for troubling reading for England fans, because they won the tournament in those years. England's largely monochrome home shirt, meanwhile, looks clean, but loses some personality in shedding the blue highlights of 2022. At least it will reflect heat well. The red away kit lends more prominence to the lion design, though the big cats look less-than-threatening with their tongues out.

  • share Rank your favourite (and least!) World Cup kits

  • He’s won at this course before. Very likable gent. Saw him play at two Ryder Cups. Very professional.

  • 3 pitch invasions in the last 4 matches from these fans...attacking the opposition players and barracking the team bus, even when they won...SFA MUST take action against these vile fans and the club they represent
  • Carrick nominated for manager of the season! And Unai Emery ISN'T!!!!!
  • Sooo corrupt
